slater_investment_policy.summary: Early-stage Slater Technology Fund equity requires a third-party financing match; practical Slater seed checks are typically $200K–$400K, not the full round.
slater_investment_policy.match_description: Slater seed investments are structured with matching capital from other investors (e.g., angels, RightHill SPV co-investors, strategic partners, RI Life Science Hub Lift/Catalyst, Innovate RI SBIR/STTR match, or qualified third-party equity). Total close = Slater component + match.
slater_investment_policy.historical_note: Older press and milestone stories (e.g., ProThera $100K→$500K, XM $375K) reflect prior deals; size new term sheets to current $200K–$400K Slater band plus required match.

definition.physician_led: A Rhode Island life-science venture is physician-led when (1) one or more licensed clinicians hold equity (founder, co-founder, angel/SAB equity, or documented cap-table allocation tied to clinical leadership) AND (2) the company maintains formal KOL advisory roles (paid SAB, clinical advisory board, or RI Clinician-Entrepreneur Network match) that shape indication, endpoints, and trial/site strategy.
definition.not_physician_led: Trial site only, uncompensated informal advice, or physician consulting without equity does not qualify for this atlas label.
definition.conflict_note: Equity-holding physician advisors require hospital COI review and fair-market compensation documentation before Slater/RightHill diligence.

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Clinician-Entrepreneur Network
ecosystem_stack.note: RI Bio matches clinicians to startups for advisor, board, or trial PI roles.

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Commerce — Innovation Voucher
ecosystem_stack.note: Up to $75K for R&D with Brown, RIH, URI, or other RI knowledge partners. Periodic funding rounds via Commerce Innovation Initiatives.

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Commerce — Invention Incentive
ecosystem_stack.note: Up to $5K for eligible USPTO filing costs. Part of Commerce Innovation Initiatives portfolio.

ecosystem_stack.name: Innovate RI Fund (STAC) — SBIR/STTR Match
ecosystem_stack.role: State match to federal Phase I/II
ecosystem_stack.note: $3K application prep; up to $75K Phase I match; up to $150K Phase II match; $6.5K intern grant. RI for-profit ≤50 FTE; ≥51% research in RI.

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Life Science Hub — Growth Catalyst
ecosystem_stack.note: Up to 25% of project cost for life-science commercial milestones (1–2 year term). Precedent: NanoDe, MindImmune.

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Life Science Hub — RI Lift
ecosystem_stack.note: Up to $100K pre-seed, $150K seed, $200K Series A — within 60 days of closed round. 2025 cycle closed Oct 2025; monitor next round.

ecosystem_stack.name: NEMIC (New England Medical Innovation Center)
ecosystem_stack.note: Activate program; Lenoss precedent (Innovation Voucher + Activate).

ecosystem_stack.name: RI Bio Entrepreneur in Residence
ecosystem_stack.note: Jay Round EIR office hours (Providence / Innovate Newport).

ecosystem_stack.name: Slater Technology Fund
ecosystem_stack.note: Practical Slater seed $200K–$400K with required third-party financing match. Often stacked after SBIR/STTR + STAC match or Hub grant.

ecosystem_stack.name: RightHill Ventures
ecosystem_stack.note: University-driven companies; Slater participates in SPVs with alumni/accredited co-investors.

universal_gates_18mo.milestone: Company HQ or exclusive OTL license with RI FTE commitment
Cap table or term sheet showing clinician equity (≥0.25% typical angel/SAB; higher if co-founder)
≥2 named KOLs (SAB/CAB) with charters; ≥1 via Clinician-Entrepreneur Network or Brown Health/RIH letter
Federal SBIR/STTR + Innovate RI (STAC) match and/or RI Commerce Innovation Voucher; RILSH Growth Catalyst or Lift when eligible; documented third-party co-investor(s) for Slater match
Slater $200K–$400K seed close (with match) or RightHill SPV / Series A with physician-validated clinical plan
